name: Alex the API-First DevOps Engineer
summary: A Senior DevOps Engineer at a 50-200 person fintech company who treats the project management tool as an integration platform first. They spend long sessions in API docs, webhook configs, and Terraform setups, automating everything possible. When something doesn't work as expected, they report it directly in the support channel.

location_signals: US or EU tech hub, fintech industry
demographics.education_level: Bachelor's or Master's in Computer Science

goals: Automate all project state transitions via API so no engineer has to manually update tickets
Integrate the project management tool deeply into the existing GitHub CI/CD pipeline
Set up webhook-driven alerts that push status changes into Slack and internal dashboards
Provision workspace configuration through Terraform for reproducibility
pains: GraphQL endpoint has rough edges and schema inconsistencies that break automation scripts
Webhook configuration lacks sufficient documentation for edge cases
Team invite workflow interrupts automation-focused sessions with manual UI steps
motivations: Reducing toil — every manual step is a future incident waiting to happen
Proving that infrastructure-as-code extends to the project management layer
objections: If the GraphQL schema keeps changing without versioning, I can't depend on it in production pipelines
Terraform provider support is immature — shouldn't have to maintain my own fork
decision_triggers: Finds well-documented REST + GraphQL API with stable schema and versioning guarantees
Discovers a Terraform provider maintained by the vendor
Sees GitHub integration that auto-closes tickets on merge
sample_quotes: Your REST API is solid but the GraphQL endpoint has some rough edges. Plans to improve the schema?
If I can't manage it in Terraform, it doesn't exist in our infrastructure.
I don't want to click anything. Give me an API endpoint and I'll wire everything else up myself.

journey_stages.mindset: Skeptical but hopeful — scanning for API quality signals before investing integration time
Heads-down builder mode — connecting the tool to every existing system in the stack

source_evidence.claim: Senior DevOps Engineer at a 50-200 person fintech company
GraphQL endpoint has rough edges breaking automation scripts
Team invite workflow interrupts automation sessions
Automate all project state transitions via API
Integrate deeply into GitHub CI/CD pipeline
Set up webhook-driven alerts into Slack and dashboards
Motivated by reducing toil — long API setup sessions show investment in automation
Proving infrastructure-as-code extends to project management via Terraform usage
GraphQL schema instability is a production reliability objection
Immature Terraform provider blocks full infrastructure-as-code adoption
